steps.title: Identify the proportional relationship
steps.content:
This is a unit rate problem: we know the total amount spent and the price per unit, and we need to find the quantity purchased. The relationship is:
$$\text{Total Cost} = \text{(Price per pound)} \times \text{(Number of pounds)}$$
Substituting the given values:
$$18 = 3 \times \text{(pounds)}$$

steps.title: Solve for the number of pounds
steps.content:
Divide both sides by \$3 to isolate the number of pounds:
$$\text{pounds} = \frac{18}{3} = 6$$
The customer purchased 6 pounds of apples.
